name: Harry Richman
description: American entertainer; singer, actor, dancer, comedian, pianist, songwriter, bandleader, and nightclub performer (1895-1972)
abstract: Harry Richman (born Henry Reichman Jr.; August 10, 1895 – November 3, 1972) was an American singer, actor, dancer, comedian, pianist, songwriter, bandleader, and nightclub performer, at his most popular in the 1920s and 1930s. In his peak years, he was one of the highest‐paid performers in show business.

concerts.label: Benefit: U.S. Convalescent Hospital at Camp Upton, L.I.

about_quotes.speaker: Albany Leon "Barney" Bigard
about_quotes.evidence: Harry Richman. All them kind of people... the boss was asking, 'If anybody wants to stay, you can make some money playing for them people.'
